title: UN Demographic Yearbook 1997 - Historical Supplement: mid-year population, vital statistics, and each-census population results, 1948-1997, all countries
countries: ALL -- files as downloaded cover every UN member state/area; see data/processed/un-demographic-yearbook-iran/ for an Iran-only convenience extraction
time_coverage: 1948-1997 (Table 1 = annual; Tables 2/3 = each national census date, which for most countries spans well before 1948 through 1997, e.g. Iran's rows run 1956-1996)
frequency: Table 1: annual. Tables 2, 3: each census (irregular, per-country). Table 9a: quinquennial/period life tables plus occasional single-year census-based estimates.
units: see each file's own header rows (population in persons; rates per 1000; life expectancy in years)
license_terms: United Nations Statistics Division -- publicly downloadable historical statistical supplement, standard UN data-reuse terms (attribution expected).
notes: MISSION CONTEXT: this is the single highest-value find of this hunt, directly resolving the previously-confirmed gap that Iran's 1956/1966/1976 census originals are not directly downloadable from Iran Data Portal or IPUMS. Table 2 ('Population by sex, residence, and intercensal rates of increase for total population, each census: 1948-1997') gives Iran's EXACT ENUMERATED census totals as officially reported by Iran to the UN Statistics Division: 1 Nov 1956 = 18,954,704 (male 9,644,944 / female 9,309,760; urban 5,953,563 = 31.4%; rural 13,001,141 = 68.6%; source code 'Cdf' = census, de facto); 1 Nov 1966 = 25,785,210; Nov 1976 = 33,708,744 (male 17,356,347 / female 16,352,397; annual intercensal rate of increase 2.7%; urban 15,854,680 = 47%; rural 17,854,064 = 53%); 22 Sep 1986 = 49,445,010 (male 25,280,961 / female 24,164,049; rate 3.8%); plus bonus 1991 (55,837,163) and 1996 (60,055,488) counts and an intercensal 1959 sample-survey count (19,745,582, source code 'Sdf' = sample, de facto). No footnote/caveat is attached to Iran's entry in Table 2 (unlike some other countries in the same table), meaning UNSD applied no special adjustment note to these figures as transcribed from Iran's own official submission. Table 3 gives the FULL AGE PYRAMID (5-year age bands, by sex, by urban/rural) for the SAME census dates plus 1959, 1971, 1975, 1981, 1984, 1991, 1994, 1996 survey/estimate years -- footnote [79] on Iran's Table 3 entry notes that for 1966 specifically, this table's total (25,078,923) covers the SETTLED population only, excluding an unsettled population of 244,141 and nomadic tribes of 462,146 (which sum to exactly the ~706,287 difference vs Table 2's all-inclusive 1966 total of 25,785,210 -- a clean internal cross-check). Table 9a gives Iran life expectancy at birth by sex and period: 1950-55 46.1yrs, 1955-60 48.3yrs, 1960-65 ~50.7yrs, 1965-70 ~53.2yrs, 1973-76 (Population Growth Survey Project, footnote [23]) 57.5yrs, 1976 census-year estimate ~55.4yrs, 1980-85 ~61yrs, 1986 ~59yrs, 1990-95 ~67.5yrs. Table 1 gives ANNUAL Iran mid-year population + crude birth rate + crude death rate + natural-increase rate for every year 1948-1997 continuously, PLUS sporadic infant-mortality-rate figures (flagged [x] in the source, meaning derived/estimated rather than direct vital-registration counts) at roughly 5-year intervals: 190 (1953), 175 (1958), 163 (1963), 145 (1968), 122 (1973), 100 (1978), 78 (1983), 52 (1988), 30.9 (1994) per 1000 live births -- this fills the 'vital statistics by decade' request from the task brief and is NOT duplicative of the existing data/processed/owid_indicators.csv coverage (OWID has child-mortality and fertility-rate for Iran but no crude birth/death rate series; confirmed by checking data/processed before this download). CROSS-VALIDATION: the 1956 and 1966 total-population figures in this table match, almost to the exact digit, both the World Bank archival-PDF citations (data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/census-demographic-citations-1956-1982/) and the Encyclopaedia Iranica CENSUS i. In Iran narrative (data/raw/iran-census/iranica-census-demography-narrative-series-1868-1998/) obtained independently in this same pass -- three-way agreement across a UN statistical compilation, a World Bank internal report, and an academic encyclopedia article gives high confidence these are the correct definitive counts. Files downloaded in full (all countries) rather than pre-filtered because that is the natural download unit UNSD provides (no API/country-filter option); see data/processed/un-demographic-yearbook-iran/ for the Iran-only convenience extraction used for actual analysis. All 4 files together are ~5.4MB, far under the 500MB size-discipline cap.
